name: Taga y Puig Estela - circular trail run from Pardines
name_original: Taga y Puig Estela - trail run circular desde Pardines
tips:
This hermitage, also known as Santa Magdalena de Pardines, has always been a place of celebrations and meetings for the people of Pardines.

Romanesque church, built in the 12th century, it was consecrated by the Bishop of Urgell in 1176. In the 18th century it was transformed by adding two side chapels. It was recently restored by the architect Joan M. de Ribot, eliminating these baroque elements in the restoration.

The function of this church was to facilitate the worship of the most remote houses of the town.

Emblematic Catalan summit for its easy and multiple ascents from all its slopes. It is located in the Serra Conivella and is separated from the Serra Cavallera by the Portella de Ogassa. From its 2040m height it offers panoramic views of the Pyrenees with the Puigmal and the Canigó, among others.

Peak of 2,013 meters that is between the municipalities of Ogassa and Pardines, in the Catalan region of Ripollès. It belongs to the Sierra Cavallera, separated from the Sierra de Conivella by the Portella de Ogassa

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Saint Mary Magdalene of Puigsac
